articles.title: The White House Wants Anthropic to Block All Jailbreaks. That May Not Be Possible
articles.subtitle: Trump administration officials tell WIRED that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Fable 5, it will need to ensure the model's guardrails can't be circumvented. Security experts say that can't be done.
articles.author: Hugo Lowell
articles.summary: Trump administration officials tell WIRED that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Fable 5, it will need to ensure the model's guardrails can't be circumvented. Security experts say that can't be done.
body: The Trump administration’s disagreement with Anthropic over its most advanced AI models appears to be fast coming to a head.

Trump officials tell Inner Loop that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Claude Fable 5, the AI model that they took offline with export controls last week over concerns about jailbreaking—a method of using prompts to get around a model’s safeguards—the company will need to take steps to actually address what the government alleges are vulnerabilities.

Anthropic has said for days that the administration’s concerns are overblown and that the effects of the jailbreaks are minimal. It reiterated this position to the Commerce Department and the Office of the National Cyber Director, Sean Cairncross, in a technical meeting on Monday.

But officials say they are past arguing whether the jailbreaks are significant, since the National Security Agency concluded that there are ways to disable guardrails on Fable 5, which are put in place to prevent users from accessing capabilities of the Mythos model related to cybersecurity, chemistry, and biology.
At this stage, the administration essentially views the situation as Anthropic’s problem to fix, according to three people familiar with discussions.

Neither the Commerce Department’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation nor the National Security Agency has the staff or the bandwidth to be drawn into chasing down every conceivable jailbreak on every model that reaches the market, the people said.

As a result, the administration believes that Anthropic should be more proactive about continually testing not just Fable 5 but all of its frontier AI models to find potential jailbreaks and flag them to the government themselves.
But on a more fundamental level, it remains unclear how Anthropic is supposed to prevent jailbreaking.

Independent cybersecurity experts have increasingly taken the view that guardrails on AI models are only a stopgap solution, since skilled users and future AI models will find ways to bypass constraints—meaning that what the White House appears to want cannot be done.

At the start of the week, Trump’s pick to serve as Acting Director of National Intelligence, Bill Pulte, was on track to never even start the job. Now, Trump has thrown him a lifeline—and it’s the permanent DNI nominee, Jay Clayton, who now faces the prospect of never serving in the role.
To recap: Trump initially named Pulte, his housing finance chief, to replace outgoing DNI Tulsi Gabbard.

Faced with bipartisan pushback because Pulte doesn’t have the national security experience required by law for the role and because he flagged allegedly questionable mortgage fraud accusations against Trump’s political enemies, Trump announced Clayton, the US attorney for the Southern District of New York, as his nominee for a permanent DNI.
keyPoints: The Trump administration’s disagreement with Anthropic over its most advanced AI models appears to be fast coming to a head.

Trump officials tell Inner Loop that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Claude Fable 5, the AI model that they took offline with export controls last week over concerns about jailbreaking—a method of using prompts to get around a model’s safeguards—the company will need to take steps to actually address what the government alleges are vulnerabilities.

Anthropic has said for days that the administration’s concerns are overblown and that the effects of the jailbreaks are minimal. It reiterated this position to the Commerce Department and the Office of the National Cyber Director, Sean Cairncross, in a technical meeting on Monday.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府与Anthropic就其最先进的人工智能模型的分歧似乎即将达到顶点。
特朗普官员告诉Inner Loop，如果Anthropic想要重新发布他们上周因担心越狱而用出口管制下线的人工智能模型克劳德·法布尔5，该公司将需要采取措施实际解决政府所声称的漏洞。
Anthropic几天来一直表示，政府的担忧被夸大了，越狱的影响微乎其微。它在周一的技术会议上向商务部和国家网络主任肖恩·凯恩克罗斯重申了这一立场。
但官员们表示，他们已经过了争论越狱是否重要的阶段，因为国家安全局得出结论，有方法可以禁用法布尔5上的护栏，这些护栏旨在防止用户访问与网络安全、化学和生物学相关的Mythos模型的能力。
在这个阶段，政府基本上将这种情况视为Anthropic需要解决的问题，据三位知情人士透露。
人们表示，商务部的人工智能标准和创新中心和国家安全局都没有人员或能力去追踪市场上每一个模型上可能出现的每一种越狱。
因此，政府认为Anthropic应该更积极地不断测试不仅仅是法布尔5，还有其所有前沿的人工智能模型，以发现潜在的越狱行为，并自行向政府报告。
但在更根本的层面上，目前尚不清楚Anthropic应该如何防止越狱。
独立的网络安全专家越来越认为，人工智能模型上的护栏只是权宜之计，因为熟练的用户和未来的人工智能模型将找到绕过限制的方法——这意味着白宫似乎想要的是无法实现的。
在本周初，特朗普选择的代理国家情报总监比尔·普尔特似乎永远不会开始这份工作。现在，特朗普给了他一根救命稻草——而永久的国家情报总监提名人杰伊·克莱顿，现在面临着永远无法担任这一角色的前景。
总结：特朗普最初任命普尔特，他的住房金融主管，来取代即将离任的国家情报总监图尔西·加巴德。
面对两党的反对，因为普尔特没有法律要求的角色所需的国家安全经验，并且他提出了针对特朗普政治敌人的可疑抵押贷款欺诈指控，特朗普宣布克莱顿，纽约南区的美国检察官，作为他永久的国家情报总监提名人。

paragraphs: The Trump administration is disagreeing with Anthropic over its most advanced AI models. Trump officials say that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Claude Fable 5, the AI model they took offline last week, the company will need to solve the jailbreak problem. Jailbreaking is a method of using prompts to get around a model’s safeguards. Anthropic says that the administration’s concerns are not serious and that jailbreaks are not a big issue. However, the National Security Agency found ways to disable guardrails on Fable 5, which are there to prevent users from accessing certain capabilities.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府正在就其最先进的人工智能模型与Anthropic公司发生分歧。特朗普官员表示，如果Anthropic想要重新发布上周他们下线的人工智能模型克劳德·费布尔5号，该公司将需要解决越狱问题。越狱是一种使用提示绕过模型安全防护的方法。Anthropic表示，政府的担忧并不严重，越狱并不是一个大问题。然而，国家安全局发现了禁用费布尔5号上的防护栏的方法，这些防护栏是用来防止用户访问某些功能的。

paragraphs: The Trump administration is in disagreement with Anthropic over its most advanced AI models. Trump officials have told Inner Loop that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Claude Fable 5, the AI model they took offline last week over concerns about jailbreaking, the company will need to address the government's allegations of vulnerabilities. Anthropic has been saying for days that the administration's concerns are exaggerated and that the effects of jailbreaks are minimal. They reiterated this position to the Commerce Department and the Office of the National Cyber Director, Sean Cairncross, in a technical meeting on Monday. However, officials say they are past arguing whether the jailbreaks are significant, since the National Security Agency concluded that there are ways to disable guardrails on Fable 5, which are put in place to prevent users from accessing capabilities of the Mythos model related to cybersecurity, chemistry, and biology. The administration essentially views the situation as Anthropic’s problem to fix, according to three people familiar with discussions. Neither the Commerce Department’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation nor the National Security Agency has the staff or the bandwidth to chase down every conceivable jailbreak on every model that reaches the market, the people said. As a result, the administration believes that Anthropic should be more proactive about continually testing not just Fable 5 but all of its frontier AI models to find potential jailbreaks and flag them to the government themselves. However, it remains unclear how Anthropic is supposed to prevent jailbreaking. Independent cybersecurity experts have increasingly taken the view that guardrails on AI models are only a stopgap solution, since skilled users and future AI models will find ways to bypass constraints—meaning that what the White House appears to want cannot be done.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府与Anthropic公司就其最先进的人工智能模型存在分歧。特朗普官员告诉Inner Loop，如果Anthropic想要重新发布上周因担心越狱问题而下线的人工智能模型克劳德·费布尔5号，该公司将需要解决政府对其安全漏洞的指控。Anthropic公司几天来一直在说，政府的担忧被夸大了，越狱的影响微乎其微。他们在周一的技术会议上向商务部和国家网络主任肖恩·凯恩克罗斯重申了这一立场。然而，官员们表示，他们已经过了争论越狱是否重要的阶段，因为国家安全局得出结论，有办法禁用费布尔5号上的防护栏，这些防护栏的设置是为了防止用户访问与网络安全、化学和生物学相关的Mythos模型的能力。根据三位熟悉讨论的人说，政府基本上将这种情况视为Anthropic需要解决的问题。商务部的人工智能标准和创新中心和国家安全局都没有足够的人员或带宽去追踪每一个上市模型的每一种可能的越狱，这些人说。因此，政府认为Anthropic应该更加主动地不断测试不仅仅是费布尔5号，还有其所有前沿的人工智能模型，以发现潜在的越狱行为，并主动向政府报告。然而，目前还不清楚Anthropic应该如何防止越狱。独立的网络安全专家越来越认为，人工智能模型上的防护栏只是一个权宜之计，因为熟练的用户和未来的人工智能模型将找到绕过限制的方法——这意味着白宫似乎想要的是无法实现的。

articles.title: Microplastics in over 75% of pet food, study finds
articles.subtitle: Researchers say certain brands and kinds of cat and dog food have more microplastics than others.
articles.author: BBC Science
articles.summary: Researchers say certain brands and kinds of cat and dog food have more microplastics than others.
body: Microplastics are in more than three-quarters of pet food products, a new study has found.
The research by the universities of Sussex and Exeter found microplastics in 16 out of the 19 brands that were tested, including "very well-known ones".
Cheaper "value range" products given to dogs, cats and wild animals contained more than the expensive ones, it found.
Microplastics are tiny plastic particles, typically smaller than 5mm.
Several studies have suggested that microplastics can harm animals and humans in many ways, though working out their exact impact is tricky.
Researchers in Sussex and Exeter said that the presence of plastic pollution in pet food may also have potential implications for health, but this was not the focus of their study.
The study found that, despite higher concentrations in dry food, wet food leads to higher daily microplastic intake because pets need to eat more of it to meet their energy requirements.

Researchers are urging government to put in new regulations to insist processed food manufacturers test for microplastic contamination - similar to the legislation that already exists to prevent chemical contamination.
"Our results are a reminder that our pets are exposed to the same chemical pollutants as ourselves," University of Exeter Prof of Ecotoxicology Tamara Galloway said.

"Pets may be inadvertently spreading plastic pollution through their food and faeces, affecting wildlife and the wider environment," Fiona Mathews, Prof of Environmental Biology at the University of Sussex, added.
Researchers said that the sources of contamination were unclear.
"Further research is now needed to find out whether ingredient quality, packaging types, or processing methods, are the main contributors to the problem," they added.
keyPoints: Microplastics are in more than three-quarters of pet food products, a new study has found.
The research by the universities of Sussex and Exeter found microplastics in 16 out of the 19 brands that were tested, including "very well-known ones".
Cheaper "value range" products given to dogs, cats and wild animals contained more than the expensive ones, it found.
paragraphTranslations: 一项新研究发现，超过四分之三的宠物食品中都含有微塑料。
萨塞克斯大学和埃克塞特大学的研究在测试的19个品牌中有16个发现了微塑料，包括“非常知名”的品牌。
研究发现，给狗、猫和野生动物的更便宜的“价值范围”产品含有的微塑料比昂贵的产品多。
微塑料是通常小于5毫米的微小塑料颗粒。
一些研究表明，微塑料可能以多种方式危害动物和人类，尽管确定它们的确切影响是困难的。
萨塞克斯和埃克塞特的研究人员表示，宠物食品中塑料污染的存在可能也对健康有潜在影响，但这不是他们研究的重点。
研究发现，尽管干食品中浓度更高，但湿食品导致每天摄入更多的微塑料，因为宠物需要吃更多的湿食品以满足它们的能量需求。
研究人员敦促政府制定新法规，要求加工食品制造商检测微塑料污染——类似于已经存在的防止化学污染的立法。
我们的研究结果提醒我们，我们的宠物和我们一样暴露在相同的化学污染物中，”埃克塞特大学生态毒理学教授塔玛拉·加洛韦说。
萨塞克斯大学环境生物学教授菲奥娜·马修斯补充说：“宠物可能通过它们的食物和粪便无意中传播塑料污染，影响野生动物和更广泛的环境。”
研究人员表示，污染的来源尚不清楚。
他们补充说：“现在需要进一步的研究，以确定成分质量、包装类型或加工方法是否是问题的主要贡献者。”

vocabulary.example: Microplastics are a big problem for the environment.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 微塑料对环境是个大问题。

vocabulary.example: The air is full of tiny particles.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 空气中充满了微小的颗粒。

vocabulary.example: Water pollution is a serious problem.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 水污染是一个严重的问题。

vocabulary.example: The food was unsafe to eat because of contamination.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 由于污染，食物不安全食用。

vocabulary.example: The company must follow strict safety regulations.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 公司必须遵守严格的安全法规。

vocabulary.example: Bacteria in human faeces can contaminate water.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 人类粪便中的细菌可以污染水。

paragraphs: A new study has found that microplastics are in over 75% of pet food products.
The research was done by the universities of Sussex and Exeter.
They tested 19 pet food brands and found microplastics in 16 of them.
Microplastics are very small plastic pieces, usually smaller than 5mm.
The study found that cheaper pet food has more microplastics than expensive pet food.
Researchers say that pet food may have health implications for pets, but they did not study this.
Wet pet food has more microplastics than dry food because pets eat more of it.
Researchers are asking for new rules to test pet food for microplastics.
paragraphTranslations: 一项新的研究发现，超过75%的宠物食品中含有微塑料。
这项研究由苏塞克斯大学和埃克塞特大学进行。
他们测试了19个宠物食品品牌，发现其中16个含有微塑料。
微塑料是非常小的塑料碎片，通常小于5毫米。
研究发现，便宜的宠物食品比贵的宠物食品含有更多的微塑料。
研究人员表示，宠物食品可能对宠物健康有影响，但他们没有研究这一点。
湿宠物食品比干宠物食品含有更多的微塑料，因为宠物吃得更多。
研究人员要求制定新规则，测试宠物食品中的微塑料。

paragraphs: Microplastics are in more than three-quarters of pet food products, a new study has found. The research by the universities of Sussex and Exeter found microplastics in 16 out of the 19 brands that were tested, including very well-known ones.

Microplastics are tiny plastic particles, typically smaller than 5mm. Several studies have suggested that microplastics can harm animals and humans in many ways, though working out their exact impact is tricky.

The study found that cheaper 'value range' products given to dogs, cats, and wild animals contained more microplastics than the expensive ones. Despite higher concentrations in dry food, wet food leads to higher daily microplastic intake because pets need to eat more of it to meet their energy requirements.

Researchers in Sussex and Exeter said that the presence of plastic pollution in pet food may also have potential implications for health, but this was not the focus of their study. The study's focus was on the presence of microplastics in pet food.

Researchers are urging the government to put in new regulations to insist processed food manufacturers test for microplastic contamination, similar to the legislation that already exists to prevent chemical contamination.

Our results are a reminder that our pets are exposed to the same chemical pollutants as ourselves," said University of Exeter Professor of Ecotoxicology Tamara Galloway. "Pets may be inadvertently spreading plastic pollution through their food and faeces, affecting wildlife and the wider environment," added Fiona Mathews, Professor of Environmental Biology at the University of Sussex.

Researchers said that the sources of contamination were unclear. "Further research is now needed to find out whether ingredient quality, packaging types, or processing methods, are the main contributors to the problem," they added.
paragraphTranslations: 一项新的研究发现，超过四分之三的宠物食品中含有微塑料。苏塞克斯大学和埃克塞特大学的研究在测试的19个品牌中发现了16个含有微塑料，包括一些非常知名的品牌。
微塑料是非常小的塑料颗粒，通常小于5毫米。一些研究表明，微塑料可能以多种方式危害动物和人类，尽管确定它们的确切影响是困难的。
研究发现，给狗、猫和野生动物的更便宜的“价值范围”产品比昂贵的产品含有更多的微塑料。尽管干食品中微塑料浓度更高，但湿食品导致每天摄入更多的微塑料，因为宠物需要吃更多的湿食品以满足它们的能量需求。
苏塞克斯和埃克塞特的研究人员表示，宠物食品中的塑料污染可能对健康有潜在影响，但这不是他们研究的重点。研究的重点是宠物食品中微塑料的存在。
研究人员敦促政府制定新法规，要求加工食品制造商测试微塑料污染，类似于已经存在的防止化学污染的法规。
我们的结果提醒我们，我们的宠物和我们自己一样暴露在相同的化学污染物中，"埃克塞特大学生态毒理学教授塔玛拉·加洛韦说。"宠物可能无意中通过它们的食物和粪便传播塑料污染，影响野生动物和更广泛的环境，"苏塞克斯大学环境生物学教授菲奥娜·马修斯补充说。
研究人员表示，污染的来源尚不清楚。"现在需要进一步的研究来找出是原料质量、包装类型还是加工方法，是问题的主要贡献者，"他们补充说。
